repo_metadata.description: Scientific critical review of the Ovoid Cavity Cosmological Model (ECM) — every claim checked against published data

discoverability.competitive_assessment: Both sites remain unindexed by Google as of migration day +1. Our position is structurally better than the dome's: we have SEO meta tags, ClaimReview JSON-LD, a brandable short URL, GSC-verification-in-progress on the new domain, GitHub repo metadata, and an apex umbrella page. The dome has an extensive AI-steering layer (llms.txt, llms-full.txt, ai_manifest.json with agent_rules, review-response.html, 12 API endpoints, synthetic daily_review.json) but has NOT pursued any search-engine submission path — so the dome's visibility is capped at whatever LLMs find via training/agent-recursion, and they have no organic third-party discussion surfacing in our checks. The dome author has also made no repo metadata moves (description, homepage, topics all null on the dome repo). Our AI-discoverability response (llms.txt documentary-vs-structural distinction, Timestamp Error tab, ClaimReview) remains sufficient; no new countermoves required this run.

Item 1 (GSC) is the highest-impact because it unblocks the new-domain indexing window. Items 2-4 are smaller but each shaves days off full discoverability.

no_action_gaps.reasoning: Our llms.txt is 59 lines and covers full methodology. Splitting into a separate extended file risks drift between the two. Revisit past 200 lines if ever warranted. Unchanged from prior runs.

This is the 'please interpret our site this way' pattern we explicitly reject. Replicating it would compromise the credibility our descriptive-not-instructional llms.txt preserves. Unchanged from prior runs.

Most exist to feed the dome's own AI-steering pipeline (daily_review.json, prediction_hashes.json, claim_index.json, etc.). We don't have a consumer pulling from our side; ClaimReview JSON-LD serves our structured-data need. Unchanged.
Meta-critique of critics; replicating leads to infinite-regress. We respond via direct evidence refutations instead. Unchanged.
Content-flood AI-steering — the pipeline regenerates a 'look how well we did today' JSON every 24 hours to appear active. Replicating damages our credibility as an independent review. Unchanged.

dome_changes_detected.assessment: The 2026-04-17 infrastructure burst (methodology.json, review-response.html, agent_rules, daily-ai-review workflow) appears to have been a one-off investment. Author has not iterated on AI-steering infrastructure in 5 days. Supports our rejection-based stance: we do not need to counter further because there is nothing new to counter.

notable_findings.finding: Google's site:funwithscience.net query still returns only the legacy /product/wimshurst-machine/ page (prior occupant of the domain). Our /dome-model-review/ path is not yet indexed on the new canonical (Day +1).
notable_findings.interpretation: Expected. First-index depends on GSC verification + sitemap submission + URL Inspection push. Realistic first-index window is 2026-05-05 to 2026-05-12 if GSC verification completes this week. Legacy Wimshurst page is a distinct URL path, will not confuse dome-model queries, and is left alone per SOCIAL-NOTE-004 item 8 ('do not touch legacy domain structure').

notable_findings.finding: Dome site also not indexed (site:john09289.github.io → zero results) despite 4+ days of continuous auto-pipeline commits.
notable_findings.interpretation: Confirms the dome author has taken no search-engine submission path. Their discoverability strategy is AI-agent-facing only (llms.txt + agent_rules + synthetic review content). This is a structural competitive advantage for us once we complete GSC verification — we will reach human searchers via Google/Bing; the dome does not, and based on the author's pattern, will not.

notable_findings.finding: Zero third-party discussion of the dome model's specific technical vocabulary surfaces across Google/Reddit/community searches. The dome's unique numerical claims (20,015 km disc radius, 8,537 km firmament peak, 5,733 km sun altitude) are not being cited or adopted by the broader flat-earth community.
notable_findings.interpretation: The dome model remains an isolated one-author project. No claim-overlap risk from derivative theories, no translations, no forks. Our review continues to face a single, static target with no community amplification.
